findingsResolved.finding: R8 swipe-down to dismiss not implemented
findingsResolved.evidence: BottomSheetContainer creates a PanResponder via useMemo (line 181), gated on enableSwipeDown (line 182) — only attached when presentation==='sheet' && !blocking (router computes this at BottomSheetRouter.tsx:139). onPanResponderRelease at line 196 measures dy against 50% of sheetHeightRef (lines 197-204) and fires onBackdropPressRef when past threshold. Spring-back if under threshold (line 210). Under reduceMotion the spring is replaced by translateY.setValue(0) (lines 206-208). Layout measurement via onLayout (lines 230-238) sets sheetHeightRef. Test swipe-down.test.tsx covers four cases: drag past 50% closes (l.142-175), blocking route skips PanResponder.create entirely (l.177-196), drag at 30% spring-backs (l.198-237), reduce-motion uses snap not spring (l.239-271).

findingsResolved.finding: R15/R16 focus capture & restore not implemented
findingsResolved.evidence: useBottomSheetRouter.open now accepts BottomSheetOpenOptions.triggerNodeHandle (useBottomSheetRouter.ts:22-24). The hook publishes the handle via publishBottomSheetTriggerCapture before dispatching OPEN (line 61). BottomSheetRouter subscribes to capture (BottomSheetRouter.tsx:76-80), stores in triggerTagRef (line 78), and on CLOSE_DONE listener (lines 87-97) calls AccessibilityInfo.setAccessibilityFocus(tag) if tag !== null. No-op when handle is null — no fake call. JSDoc at useBottomSheetRouter.ts:10-21 and bottomSheetStore.ts:52-63 explicitly documents that RN 0.83 has no API to auto-capture focused element for arbitrary views, so the call-site opts in — UFR-013 honesty preserved. Test focus-restore.test.tsx asserts: round-trip 4242→4242 (l.35-72), no setAccessibilityFocus when handle omitted (l.74-103), last-write-wins overwrites prior handle (l.105-154).

findingsResolved.finding: R12 sequencing collapsed into synchronous reducer chain
findingsResolved.evidence: dispatchBottomSheetEvent (bottomSheetStore.ts:81-101) no longer auto-fires OPEN_DONE/CLOSE_DONE. The reducer (bottomSheetMachine.ts) only consumes these events. BottomSheetContainer dispatches OPEN_DONE inside Animated.parallel(...).start(({finished}) => …) at line 128 once entrance settles, and CLOSE_DONE at line 162 once exit settles. Under reduceMotion the dispatch is synchronous (lines 110, 146) — deterministic snap path preserved (spec R13). Test sequencing.test.tsx asserts: opening state persists until Animated.timing callback fires (l.32-60), exit anim keeps content mounted while CLOSE in flight (l.62-92), queued route mounts only AFTER first route's exit anim drains (l.94-134).

findingsResolved.finding: Dead expression {blocking ? null : null} + zombie prop
findingsResolved.evidence: BottomSheetContainerProps (lines 21-41) no longer destructures `blocking`. The prop has been replaced by a computed `enableSwipeDown: boolean` that the router pre-resolves (BottomSheetRouter.tsx:139: `definition.presentation === 'sheet' && !definition.blocking`). The dead `{blocking ? null : null}` JSX line is gone — full file walk shows no such expression. JSDoc at lines 23-29 explains that the container itself never reads `blocking`. Doctrine feedback_bury_dead_code respected.

nice-to-have.issue: Module-level mutable `state` / `listeners` set (carried over from boucle 1 nice-to-have). Trade-off accepted by the team — hook works without provider wrapping. resetBottomSheetState() on router unmount + jest.clearAllMocks() in beforeEach keep tests isolated. Not blocking.
nice-to-have.suggestion: Optional follow-up: migrate to React.createContext provider as spec §2.6 originally suggested, OR document the deliberate module-global pattern in `BottomSheetRouter.tsx` JSDoc. Defer to post-launch unless test-suite parallelism becomes an issue.

nice-to-have.issue: Lazy wrapper for the browser route — carried over from boucle 1 nice-to-have. Justified (defers react-native-webview native import for jest), already commented with eslint-disable + Approved-by.
nice-to-have.suggestion: Optional: add a one-liner comment in routes.ts near the import (line 12) explaining why this route alone uses a lazy wrapper, for future maintainers.

nice-to-have.issue: Carried over: integration test still mocks the router barrel instead of rendering the real BottomSheetRouter. Spec §3 T8.2 preferred the real render. Integration coverage between screen effects and router state machine is shallower than ideal.
nice-to-have.suggestion: Optional follow-up: add a single end-to-end test rendering real BottomSheetRouter + ChatSessionScreen, complementing the current mocked path.

summary: Boucle 2 fresh-context review: les 4 findings important de la boucle 1 sont tous RESOLVED. (1) R8 swipe-down: PanResponder gated par enableSwipeDown (calcule par router), seuil 50% via onLayout, spring-back ou snap reduce-motion, 4 cas testes dans swipe-down.test.tsx. (2) R15/R16 focus restore: BottomSheetOpenOptions.triggerNodeHandle opt-in honnete (JSDoc explique limite RN 0.83), capture via publishBottomSheetTriggerCapture avant OPEN, restore sur CLOSE_DONE listener, no-op si null, 3 cas testes dans focus-restore.test.tsx. (3) R12 sequencing: OPEN_DONE/CLOSE_DONE dispatchs depuis Animated.timing(...).start(callback) seulement, plus depuis le store; reduce-motion = dispatch synchrone deterministe; queued route monte uniquement apres exit anim settled, 3 cas testes dans sequencing.test.tsx. (4) Dead expression: prop blocking supprime de BottomSheetContainerProps, remplace par enableSwipeDown pre-calcule. Tests: 211 suites / 2145 tests PASS, tsc PASS, lint PASS. Gates Musaium tous au vert. Aucune regression detectee. Score 90 vs 83 (+7), verdict APPROVED. Trois nice-to-have herites (store module-global, lazy browser wrapper, integration test mockee) restent ouverts mais non bloquants.
